title: Chapter Ninety-Five: The Divine Sword Squad
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The Namgung family had surged forward when Se-jin took the killing blow. Not because of his last command -- they would have ignored that. They stopped because twenty warriors in mismatched gear appeared between the boy and the blades: Desolate Fortress' Divine Sword Squad.

First among seventeen military forces under the Divine Sword Group. First in founding, first in martial might.

"He won from that distance," one of them said.

"The way he switches between sword and fist -- that's talent."

"Unless one has considerable protective energy, it'd be instant death. Like Shaolin's Hundred Step Divine Fist condensed into close-range."

"The preparation time is long, though. Not precise. Massive recoil. Relies entirely on instinct."

"Even so, Sword Uncle -- the power alone is on par with a divine technique."

Their composition was strange. Men and women, young and old. A girl in black robes with a spear on her back grinned at the retreating Namgung warriors. "Look at them. Flinching."

"Grandmother Evil's energy waves are strong," a handsome middle-aged man said, calling the girl *grandmother* without irony, "but it's probably not that. They're the Namgung family, after all."

A comet of the murim had been extinguished. Even among the Divine Sword Squad, silence fell for Namgung Se-jin.

"One worthy of mourning."

"Such a waste."

"More than that -- Lightning Genius seems like he wants to stay on."

Yeon-shin stood where the fight had ended. He had not sheathed his sword. He was watching Namgung warriors collect Se-jin's body.

*How should one live as a martial artist?*

He had always prioritised his lifespan. But was survival alone a worthy banner?

The girl in black -- Yue Shou-lin, Divine Spear of the Wild, a master who had reversed her body's years through pure refinement -- clicked her tongue. "He's not showing fighting spirit. That's recklessness. Can't think straight. Fallen into melancholy after defeating a worthy opponent. Common at that age."

She put both hands to her lips. Tremendous energy waves burst forth.

"CHILD! COME HERE!"

An explosion no less powerful than Brilliant River itself. Yeon-shin's head jerked up. The black-robed girl wore an irritating smile.

"You must be aware of your position! You struck down the Namgung young master! Those Namgung warriors over there -- their eyes are so wide they might come slashing at any moment!"

He blinked. "Ah."

*Troublesome.* He had wanted to savour the enlightenment Se-jin had bestowed.

"Next," he said instead, turning toward the Namgung side. "Come forth."

He would not follow a dead woman's scheme. Namgung Mi had designed the format so the winner could surrender after two matches -- rely on rank differences to protect life and honour. He would not.

*I will not show a shameful appearance before Se-jin's body.*

An Azure Sky Sword Heaven Group Leader leaped forward with a great shout. Middle-aged, blade-like eyes, blue martial robes. He landed before Yeon-shin with an impact that sent sand stinging into his face.

The man's energy waves were tremendous. True qi undulating from every pore in waves. A veteran supreme master.

*If I could just land the Brilliant River--*

"Stop there."

A clear voice. The Group Leader's eyes widened before Yeon-shin could even turn.

"Divine Sword Group Leader!"

Behind Yeon-shin, a presence sprouted -- clear enough to make his senses shiver. He turned despite facing an enemy.

A tall man. Scholar's impression. Scholar's bearing. Purple robe instead of scholar's cloth. His power, fully revealed now, was transcendent.

"Sword Heaven Group Leader," Ma Yeon-jeok's grandson said quietly. "That's enough."

"How can you be so shameless--"

"The one without shame is you. Trying to harm a boy so much younger."

He walked forward. No one stopped him. The Namgung Family Head glared from across the valley, stroking his sword hilt. The Azure Sky Sword Heaven Group Leader's momentum vanished as if swept away by water.

"They called you Lightning Genius?" The Divine Sword Group Leader looked down at Yeon-shin with faint amusement.

"I greet the Group Leader."

"You -- I thought your eyes were faulty. Until arriving here, you treated your Group Leader as a non-existent person. An unfamiliar experience." A slight smile. "Withdraw now. You've fought two life-and-death matches and you're far too spent for the next. I understand your resolve, but words alone won't do. So I'll add this: I heard from the Grand Administrator that I must pass down a strand of my martial arts to you. Let me build you a murim friendship that can become your strength."

Yeon-shin was silent. How could he surrender before Se-jin?

The Divine Sword Group Leader made a deliberate expression -- brow furrowed, almost comically pained. "You have already demonstrated more than enough courage. Now consider your superior."

*I didn't know the Group Leader was this eccentric.*

"I'll take that as agreement."

He smiled broadly and gently pushed Yeon-shin's shoulder aside. Yue Shou-lin had already moved -- a blur of speed -- and struck Namgung Hwa-shin's nape before the young man could even register surprise. He crumpled.

"White Qilin is in no state for matches," the Group Leader announced. "Count that as our loss. Next it's my turn."

He stepped forward alone.

"From now on, I will step forward for all martial matches with Namgung."

Yeon-shin watched the tall man walk into the arena as easily as one enters a garden.

But the boy was not watching the coming battle. He was already sinking inward, chasing something Se-jin had given him -- an insight that burned in his meridians like a river of light.

*Brilliant River.*

A name given by a dying man. A technique born between two swords. The fourth move of Eternal Blossom Fist, crystallised in the moment of a worthy opponent's last breath.

He sat in the dust where the fight had ended and closed his eyes. He was dimly aware of the Group Leader's footsteps growing distant, of the Namgung Family Head drawing his sword at last, of the vast murmur of a thousand spectators holding their breath.

*Should not dwell anywhere and thus give rise to mind.*

He let it all fall away.

Behind him, three Radiant Demon Wing scouts knelt before Yue Shou-lin. Their faces were grim.

"Vice Leader. Reports from the eastern approach."

"Speak."

"Evil sect forces. Assembling at the border of South Zhili. Deep Martial Alliance banners confirmed. Blood Flame Sect runners among them."

The girl with a child's face looked toward the horizon where storm clouds gathered thick and dark, swallowing the afternoon sun.

"How many?"

"At least three hundred confirmed. Possibly more behind the ridgeline. They aren't hiding."

She was quiet for a long breath.

"Let them come," Yue Shou-lin said.

But her hand rested on the spear across her back, and she did not take her eyes from the darkening east.
